{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Officer",
  "description": "PG Consulting Limited is a recruitment, training and business consulting firm. We provide our clients - businesses and corporate organizations - across different industries with superior human capital, high-quality trainings and cutting-edge consulting to help them drive organizational growth, multiply staff productivity, gain appropriate market positioning and maximise profit.

Over the course of almost 20 years of our existence, we have serviced various organizations, many of which are strong indigenous brands operating with global standards.We are most inclined to building strong brands and supporting business growth across industries. Our preference is to work with our clients as partners on an on-going basis towards making their organizations internally effective and more profitable.As we grow into the future, the sustaining core of our operations is working with you, all the way, from People to Profit.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a skilled and customer-focused professional to join our team as a Customer Support Officer.
	The ideal candidate will have experience in customer support roles, demonstrating strong communication skills, problem-solving abilities, and a commitment to delivering exceptional service.


Responsibilities


	Address customer inquiries, concerns, and requests with a focus on delivering a positive experience.
	Identify and resolve customer issues by actively listening, analyzing problems, and implementing appropriate solutions.
	Stay updated on product features, updates, and troubleshooting procedures.
	Communicate effectively with customers using clear and concise language.
	Maintain accurate and detailed records of customer interactions, feedback, and resolutions in the customer support database.
	Monitor customer satisfaction levels and proactively seek feedback to identify areas for improvement.
	Collaborate with other departments, such as sales, product development, and marketing, to share customer insights and contribute to overall business improvement.


Requirements


	B.Sc / HND in any relevant field.
	Minimum of 2 to 3 years of experience in a customer support role.
	Familiarity with customer support tools and systems.
	Excellent problem-solving skills and a proactive approach to addressing customer needs.
	Strong verbal and written communication skills.
	Ability to work efficiently in a team and independently.
	Empathy and patience in dealing with customer inquiries and concerns.


Salary
N150,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp; jobs@pgconsultinguk.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-05",
  "validThrough": "2026-03-16",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "PG Consulting"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Lagos",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "195508"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 150000,
      "maxValue": 250000,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }
  }
}



Customer Service Officer Job at PG Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        PG Consulting Limited is a recruitment, training and business consulting firm. We provide our clients - businesses and corporate organizations - across different industries with superior human capital, high-quality trainings and cutting-edge consulting to help them drive organizational growth, multiply staff productivity, gain appropriate market positioning and maximise profit.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Over the course of almost 20 years of our existence, we have serviced various organizations, many of which are strong indigenous brands operating with global standards.We are most inclined to building strong brands and supporting business growth across industries. Our preference is to work with our clients as partners on an on-going basis towards making their organizations internally effective and more profitable.As we grow into the future, the sustaining core of our operations is working with you, all the way, from People to Profit.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a skilled and customer-focused professional to join our team as a Customer Support Officer.
	The ideal candidate will have experience in customer support roles, demonstrating strong communication skills, problem-solving abilities, and a commitment to delivering exceptional service.


Responsibilities


	Address customer inquiries, concerns, and requests with a focus on delivering a positive experience.
	Identify and resolve customer issues by actively listening, analyzing problems, and implementing appropriate solutions.
	Stay updated on product features, updates, and troubleshooting procedures.
	Communicate effectively with customers using clear and concise language.
	Maintain accurate and detailed records of customer interactions, feedback, and resolutions in the customer support database.
	Monitor customer satisfaction levels and proactively seek feedback to identify areas for improvement.
	Collaborate with other departments, such as sales, product development, and marketing, to share customer insights and contribute to overall business improvement.


Requirements


	B.Sc / HND in any relevant field.
	Minimum of 2 to 3 years of experience in a customer support role.
	Familiarity with customer support tools and systems.
	Excellent problem-solving skills and a proactive approach to addressing customer needs.
	Strong verbal and written communication skills.
	Ability to work efficiently in a team and independently.
	Empathy and patience in dealing with customer inquiries and concerns.


Salary
N150,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:  jobs@pgconsultinguk.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Fleet Officer",
  "description": "Aquila introduced the finance lease product at the origination of the company in 2005 and has since then developed tailor-made solutions for its diverse customer segments. The product is offered to retail clients (both individually and via associations and cooperatives schemes), small and medium- sized enterprises and small corporate.

An active collaboration of the agency is required to structure the transaction and ensure prevention of over-indebtedness by implementing checks on the client&rsquo;s disposable income. To facilitate repayments, these are made directly from source.

This innovative scheme has so far provided access to equipment finance to close to 25,000 Nigerians. Typical assets which Aquila finances under this scheme are motorcycles, tricycles, laptops, refrigerators, freezers, and generators which support the generation of additional income to supplement the incomes of lower-ranking Nig

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Fleet Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Build and maintain a standardized fleet with supplier and field to determine appropriate equipment / truck / trailer specifications and options
	Monitors orders to completion.
	Works with Suppliers on revised delivery dates and to expedite product shipments and ensure on-time delivery.
	Research and update current specifications and quotes on intranet excel file.
	Attends supplier/ clients&rsquo; meetings as directed by management.
	Identifies and documents cost savings opportunities, such as researching different deals on assets leasing to obtain better package pricing.
	Assist purchasing department with vehicle specifications, orders, and vendor issues. Vehicle Administration: &middot; Manages system to ensure data integrity within rentals and vendors.
	Maintains and monitors vendor performance standards.
	Process vehicle change forms from Vehicle Fleet email box for location changes, driver changes. Submit changes to leasing company vendor.
	Validates units billed on leasing invoices. Contact branch and clients on discrepancies and continue through to resolution.
	Prepares leasing invoices for approvals and payment.
	Administers vehicle title change processes.
	Acts as liaison between clients in resolving issues and coordinating vehicle acquisitions, disposals and services. &middot; Prepares and submits expense allocation files to Fixed Assets.
	Assists field and region locations with:
	
		Order Status
		Requisition tool
		Vehicle/Trailer specifications
		Fuel Cards
		Tracking and documentation
		Licensing, Titling and Registrations
		Vehicle Disposal
		Policies and Procedures
		Allocated expenses&nbsp;
		Vehicle Leases
	
	
	Resolves issues between suppliers and field locations
	Responds to requests from inquiries from regions and corporate departments


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to:&nbsp;humancapital@aquilagroupng.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Fleet Officer Job at Aquila Partners




        
    

        
        Aquila introduced the finance lease product at the origination of the company in 2005 and has since then developed tailor-made solutions for its diverse customer segments. The product is offered to retail clients (both individually and via associations and cooperatives schemes), small and medium- sized enterprises and small corporate.

An active collaboration of the agency is required to structure the transaction and ensure prevention of over-indebtedness by implementing checks on the client’s disposable income. To facilitate repayments, these are made directly from source.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



This innovative scheme has so far provided access to equipment finance to close to 25,000 Nigerians. Typical assets which Aquila finances under this scheme are motorcycles, tricycles, laptops, refrigerators, freezers, and generators which support the generation of additional income to supplement the incomes of lower-ranking Nig

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Fleet Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Build and maintain a standardized fleet with supplier and field to determine appropriate equipment / truck / trailer specifications and options
	Monitors orders to completion.
	Works with Suppliers on revised delivery dates and to expedite product shipments and ensure on-time delivery.
	Research and update current specifications and quotes on intranet excel file.
	Attends supplier/ clients’ meetings as directed by management.
	Identifies and documents cost savings opportunities, such as researching different deals on assets leasing to obtain better package pricing.
	Assist purchasing department with vehicle specifications, orders, and vendor issues. Vehicle Administration: · Manages system to ensure data integrity within rentals and vendors.
	Maintains and monitors vendor performance standards.
	Process vehicle change forms from Vehicle Fleet email box for location changes, driver changes. Submit changes to leasing company vendor.
	Validates units billed on leasing invoices. Contact branch and clients on discrepancies and continue through to resolution.
	Prepares leasing invoices for approvals and payment.
	Administers vehicle title change processes.
	Acts as liaison between clients in resolving issues and coordinating vehicle acquisitions, disposals and services. · Prepares and submits expense allocation files to Fixed Assets.
	Assists field and region locations with:
	
		Order Status
		Requisition tool
		Vehicle/Trailer specifications
		Fuel Cards
		Tracking and documentation
		Licensing, Titling and Registrations
		Vehicle Disposal
		Policies and Procedures
		Allocated expenses 
		Vehicle Leases
	
	
	Resolves issues between suppliers and field locations
	Responds to requests from inquiries from regions and corporate departments


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: humancapital@aquilagroupng.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

Inventory Officer Job at Eko Maintenance Limited




        
    

        
        Eko Maintenance Limited is a rapidly growing Facility Management Company that specializes in the maintenance of high-rise building facilities with an office located in Victoria Island, Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Officer
Job Location: Victoria Island,  Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Receive incoming goods, store them appropriately, and ensure they are issued correctly.
	Monitor and manage stock levels to ensure adequate supplies are available.
	Distribute supplies from stock as needed by different departments.
	Keep accurate stock records using both manual and computerized systems.
	Use Excel sheets to coordinate and track inventory functions.
	Monitor the movement of materials and products in and out of the inventory through accurate record-keeping.
	Keep track of goods and supplies within the store, ensuring everything is properly organized and accessible.


Relevant Work Experience / Qualification


	Bachelor’s Degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Business Administration, Supply Chain Management or related field.
	1 – 3 years of working experience in a related field.
	Proficiency in inventory software, ERP system, Microsoft Suite ( Excel, word, Outlook).


Benefit


	HMO Coverage
	Pension
	Annual Leave


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment3@ekomaintenance.com using "Inventory Officer" as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Client Service Officer (Southwest)",
  "description": "Stanbic IBTC Bank is a leading African banking group focused on emerging markets globally. It has been a mainstay of South Africa&#39;s financial system for 150 years, and now spans 16 countries across the African continent.

Standard Bank is a firm believer in technical innovation, to help us guarantee exceptional client service and leading edge financial solutions. Our growing global success reflects our commitment to the latest solutions, the best people, and a uniquely flexible and vibrant working culture. To help us drive our success into the future, we are looking for resourceful individuals to join our dedicated team at our offices.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Client Service Officer (Southwest)

Job Ref ID:&nbsp;80448655A-0001
Job Location:&nbsp;Osun, Ekiti, Ondo and Ogun

Job Description


	To ensure that customer service requests, complaints and queries received are acknowledged, understood and efficiently resolved within specified timelines or escalated to the appropriate area in order to deliver a positive client experience.
	To be responsible for providing specialized support and resolving complex queries and issues that frontline support personnel are unable to address and therefore can be the first point of escalation for clients and frontline staff.&nbsp;


Key Responsibilities


	Attend to telephonic queries regarding cheque books.
	Receive and handle ATM cards according to laid-down policies and procedures&nbsp;and perform daily balancing of ATM cards in conjunction with the Asset Custodian.
	Issue off-cycle statements at customer&rsquo;s instance and take charges as appropriate and keep records of all source instructions from customers.
	Identify cross-selling and migration opportunities and sell products/services reactively and participate in tactical sales/marketing activities as required.
	&nbsp;Attend to the posting of all value transactions within own level of authority and advice Team Leader Frontline Support to verify all value transactions posted.
	Process instructions for Cash Transfers (CT) according to laid-down policy, complete Customer Complaints Register and obtain source documents where applicable and log completed Customer Complaints on the appropriate Remedy Application on daily basis.


Qualifications


	Minimum of First Degree.


Experience Required:


	1 - 2 years of experience with the bank&rsquo;s products, compliance procedures, and regulations governing financial services management.
	Previous experience in branch banking is required.


Behavioural Competencies:


	Articulating Information
	Embracing Change
	Establishing Rapport
	Following Procedures
	Managing Tasks
	Meeting Timescales
	Producing Output
	Seizing Opportunities
	Showing Composure
	Team Working
	Upholding Standards
	Valuing Individuals.


Technical Competencies:


	Active Listening
	Banking Process &amp; Procedures
	Customer Understanding (Consumer Banking)
	Product and Services Knowledge
	Product Knowledge (Consumer Banking)
	Risk Identification
	Telephone Caller Handling.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-05",
  "validThrough": "2026-04-06",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Stanbic IBTC Bank"
    
  },

    "@type": "Place",

      "@type": "PostalAddress",

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },

  },

Client Service Officer (Southwest) Job at Stanbic IBTC Bank




        
    

        
        Stanbic IBTC Bank is a leading African banking group focused on emerging markets globally. It has been a mainstay of South Africa's financial system for 150 years, and now spans 16 countries across the African continent.

Standard Bank is a firm believer in technical innovation, to help us guarantee exceptional client service and leading edge financial solutions. Our growing global success reflects our commitment to the latest solutions, the best people, and a uniquely flexible and vibrant working culture. To help us drive our success into the future, we are looking for resourceful individuals to join our dedicated team at our offices.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Client Service Officer (Southwest)
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Ref ID: 80448655A-0001
Job Location: Osun, Ekiti, Ondo and Ogun

Job Description


	To ensure that customer service requests, complaints and queries received are acknowledged, understood and efficiently resolved within specified timelines or escalated to the appropriate area in order to deliver a positive client experience.
	To be responsible for providing specialized support and resolving complex queries and issues that frontline support personnel are unable to address and therefore can be the first point of escalation for clients and frontline staff. 


Key Responsibilities


	Attend to telephonic queries regarding cheque books.
	Receive and handle ATM cards according to laid-down policies and procedures and perform daily balancing of ATM cards in conjunction with the Asset Custodian.
	Issue off-cycle statements at customer’s instance and take charges as appropriate and keep records of all source instructions from customers.
	Identify cross-selling and migration opportunities and sell products/services reactively and participate in tactical sales/marketing activities as required.
	 Attend to the posting of all value transactions within own level of authority and advice Team Leader Frontline Support to verify all value transactions posted.
	Process instructions for Cash Transfers (CT) according to laid-down policy, complete Customer Complaints Register and obtain source documents where applicable and log completed Customer Complaints on the appropriate Remedy Application on daily basis.


Qualifications


	Minimum of First Degree.


Experience Required:


	1 - 2 years of experience with the bank’s products, compliance procedures, and regulations governing financial services management.
	Previous experience in branch banking is required.


Behavioural Competencies:


	Articulating Information
	Embracing Change
	Establishing Rapport
	Following Procedures
	Managing Tasks
	Meeting Timescales
	Producing Output
	Seizing Opportunities
	Showing Composure
	Team Working
	Upholding Standards
	Valuing Individuals.


Technical Competencies:


	Active Listening
	Banking Process & Procedures
	Customer Understanding (Consumer Banking)
	Product and Services Knowledge
	Product Knowledge (Consumer Banking)
	Risk Identification
	Telephone Caller Handling.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

	We are seeking an experienced Finance Officer with strong expertise in tax compliance, auditing, and financial reporting in Nigeria.

	In-depth knowledge ofNigerian tax laws and regulations
	Ability to liaise effectively with tax authorities and auditors
	Has experience working with government bodies.
	Have a good understanding of royalty payments.


Benefits
Salary is very attractive.

Method of Application

Finance Officer Job at Ganfeng Lithium Industry Limited




        
    

        
        Ganfeng Lithium Industry Limited is a mining company engaged in the exploration, mining, and processing of lithium. With a strong presence across the country, the company operates branches in Lagos, Abuja, and Nasarawa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Officer

Job Location:  Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced Finance Officer with strong expertise in tax compliance, auditing, and financial reporting in Nigeria.


Qualifications
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field (ICAN/ACCA is an added advantage).


Key Requirements:


	Minimum of 5 years relevant experienceas a Finance Officer or similar role
	Proven experience handlingTax Clearance Certificates (TCC)
	Strong knowledge ofauditing processesand financial controls
	Hands-on experience withtax filing and remittanceto government offices (FIRS, State IRS, PAYE, VAT, WHT, etc.)
	In-depth knowledge ofNigerian tax laws and regulations
	Ability to liaise effectively with tax authorities and auditors
	Has experience working with government bodies.
	Have a good understanding of royalty payments.


Benefits
Salary is very attractive.
